title: The Fretboard: Notes, Strings, and Navigation
description: Learn the layout of the bass fretboard — standard tuning, natural notes on every string, octave shapes, and how to find any note in multiple positions. Includes the bassist's role in an ensemble.
learningGoals: Name all four open strings instantly
Find all natural notes on the E and A strings up to fret 12
Use the octave shape to move between strings
Describe the bassist's role in a band

title: Rhythm Fundamentals for Bass
description: The rhythmic building blocks every bassist needs: note values, time signatures, counting subdivisions, rests, and most importantly — how to lock with the kick drum.
learningGoals: Count quarter, eighth, and sixteenth note subdivisions out loud
Play a steady quarter-note root groove with a metronome
Play a steady eighth-note root groove with a metronome
Understand how the bass locks with the kick drum
Play a basic syncopated pattern

title: Intervals: The Language of Distance
description: Understand the 12 intervals, how they look on the fretboard (both on one string and across strings), how they sound, and why the perfect fourth and fifth are so central to bass playing.
learningGoals: Name and count semitones for all 12 intervals
Find any interval from a given root on one string
Play the perfect fifth shape across all string pairs
Play a root-fifth groove over a I-IV-V progression

title: The Major Scale and Diatonic Harmony
description: The major scale formula, playing it in position and across strings, diatonic chords I–VII, Roman numeral notation, and the Nashville Number System.
learningGoals: Play the major scale in at least two positions
Name the diatonic chords in any major key
Read a simple chord chart using Roman numerals

title: The Minor World: Natural, Pentatonic, and Blues
description: Natural minor scale, minor pentatonic, the blues scale, relative major/minor relationships, and how to navigate charts that mix major and minor chords.
learningGoals: Play the natural minor scale
Play the minor pentatonic box position
Play the blues scale and identify the blue note
Identify the relative minor of any major key

title: Groove Architecture: Funk, R&B, and Rock
description: Root-fifth-octave patterns, chord tone outlining, 16th-note funk grooves, ghost notes, and the Motown eighth-note feel. How to support a vocalist and leave space for soloists.
learningGoals: Play a root-fifth-octave groove in any key
Play a 16th-note funk pattern with ghost notes
Outline chord tones over a I-IV-V progression
Play a Motown-style eighth-note bass line

title: Walking Bass Lines: Jazz and Blues
description: The 12-bar blues, ii-V-I progressions, quarter-note walking bass principles, guide tones (3rds and 7ths), and chromatic approach notes. How to comp behind a soloist.
learningGoals: Play a simple bass line over a 12-bar blues
Identify and play ii-V-I in at least two keys
Construct a 4-bar walking bass line using guide tones
Use chromatic approach notes to connect chord tones
